paragraphs.text: SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS SPECIFICATION
Software Requirements Specification (SRS)
ONLINE LEARNING SYSTEM
(BEE ACADEMY – Online Learning Platform)
Version History
Table of Contents
1 INTRODUCTION
1.1. Purpose

This document describes the functional and non-functional requirements for the Bee Academy online learning platform. It serves as a common basis for the development team, supports the testing team in building test cases (Unit, Integration, System), and acts as a reference for scope confirmation with stakeholders throughout the SDLC.
1.2. System Scope

Bee Academy operates a one-time-purchase course marketplace model. Students purchase courses and learn at their own pace; the system records revenue splits, and the Admin transfers the teachers' revenue shares on a periodic basis.

Student Portal: search for and purchase courses; pay via PayOS; study videos/documents; take notes; complete Quizzes, assignments, and 4 Exams; earn points from Exam results; redeem Reward Vouchers; track progress; receive preliminary AI grading/attempt analysis; view/download certificates.
Parent Portal: send link invitations, monitor and export progress reports, view the child's payment history, and communicate with Teachers. Parents cannot create Orders in the current codebase.

Teacher Portal: manage courses/chapters/lessons, Quizzes/Exams, the question bank, and questions; grade submissions; handle retakes; answer Q&A; manage bank accounts using OTP; view revenue and payouts.
Admin Panel: create/reissue Teacher accounts, block/unblock accounts, approve courses, handle complaints, manually confirm payouts, view dashboards/analytics, and send in-app notifications.
Technology baseline: frontend React 19 + Vite + TypeScript; backend Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 REST MVC; PostgreSQL, GoTrue Auth/JWT, and Storage on Supabase; PayOS; SMTP; Google Gemini API.

Out of Scope: livestreaming, fixed class schedules, automatic transfers to Teachers, Parents purchasing courses on behalf of children, automatic refunds, and functions that have no endpoint/service in the develop baseline.
1.3. Definitions and Abbreviations
1.4. Reference Documents
IEEE Std 830-1998: Recommended Practice for Software Requirements Specifications.
ISO/IEC 25010:2011: Systems and software Quality Requirements and Evaluation (SQuaRE).
OWASP Testing Guide v4.2.
OWASP Top 10:2025: The Ten Most Critical Web Application Security Risks (https://owasp.org/Top10/2025/).

Internal document: "Bee Academy - Use Case Specification, version 6.5".
Reference source code: SUMMER2026SE/swp391-rbl-project-se20a01_team3, develop branch at commit 209b0e98a4d04028ed76dcdaa1551ad6be179542, together with the isBlocked fix in the login/JWT filter confirmed by the development team.
1.5. Document Structure Overview
This document is organized as follows:
Section 2 describes the system overview, operating environment, external interfaces, constraints, and assumptions;
Section 3 specifies the functional requirements in detail across 8 modules with a total of 47 use cases;
Section 4 specifies the non-functional requirements;
Section 5 presents the test traceability matrix following the BR -> UC -> REQ -> Test Case chain and provides guidance for deriving test cases from the SRS.
Section 6 presents supplementary control appendices added after review, including BR conventions, RBAC, state machines, business rules, and external interface contracts.

Section 7 completes the SRS-template appendices with code-backed system-boundary/use-case diagrams, screen flows and catalog, screen authorization, non-screen functions, an ERD/entity catalog, screen-content rules, common requirements, application messages, and other implementation constraints.
2 GENERAL SYSTEM DESCRIPTION
2.1. Product Context

Bee Academy is a B2C EdTech platform for Vietnamese lower-secondary students. The current code baseline is a full-stack web application: a React 19/Vite/TypeScript frontend and a Spring Boot 3.2 backend using REST MVC architecture; data, authentication, and storage use Supabase; payments use PayOS; email uses SMTP; and AI uses the Google Gemini API. This document does not describe the system as using microservices/Kubernetes/CDN when those components have not been implemented in the develop source code.
2.2. Main Functions

Account management: Students/Parents self-register using a 5-minute OTP; Teacher accounts are created by the Admin with a temporary password; login/refresh/logout are handled through Supabase; password reset and password change; profile/avatar management; Parent-Student linking.
Search and discover PUBLISHED courses: filter by subject, grade, keyword, featured status, price, and rating; pagination/sorting; view details by UUID/slug and isFree lessons.

Purchase and payment: only Students create Orders; PayOS create-link/webhook/verify/reconcile/cancel; Reward Points are earned only from Exams and used for Reward Vouchers; users view their own Order history; thread-based complaints support file attachments.

Self-paced learning: videos delivered via signed URLs, unique watched segments recorded, and completion at 90%; notes; document download through a one-time token; assignments, Quizzes, and 4 Exams; progress/PDF; PDF/QR certificates.

Interaction and support: thread-based Q&A between Students and Teachers; stateless Gemini chat, a 4-week roadmap based on actual progress, preliminary AI grading of Exams, and Quiz result analysis for Students.
Parents: send/cancel link invitations; Students accept/reject; either party can unlink; monitor progress, export XLSX reports, view payment history, and contact Teachers.

Teachers: CRUD and ordering for courses/chapters/lessons; version migration; Quizzes/4 Exams; question bank/questions/version/audit/bulk operations; grade assignments/exams; retakes; Q&A; revenue/payout; change bank accounts using OTP.

Administration: consolidated dashboard; analytics for revenue/enrollment/user/course-category trends; user list; create/reissue temporary passwords for Teachers; block/unblock; Teacher approval status; course approval; complaint handling; view/confirm payouts; and broadcast in-app notifications.

Certificates: when all progress items are complete and all 4 exam slots 0-3 are PASSED, issue a PDF with QR code/watermark; signed view/download URL valid for 600 seconds; public verification; score corrections may result in NEEDS_REVIEW, REISSUED, or REVOKED.
2.3. User Classification (Actors)
2.4. Operating Environment

Note: this section describes the baseline implemented in the develop branch. Production targets that cannot be verified directly from the code are explicitly identified as testing objectives and must not be considered implemented functionality.

Client: React 19, Vite, TypeScript 5, and Tailwind CSS 4; responsive design is handled by the frontend. Specific browser-version support is a compatibility-testing objective and is not hard-coded in the backend.
Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3.2, Spring MVC, Spring Security, Spring Data JPA/Hibernate; stateless APIs use Supabase JWT.

Data: Supabase PostgreSQL; authentication: Supabase GoTrue; files: Supabase Storage. Private videos are provided through signed URLs valid for 3600 seconds; certificates use signed URLs valid for 600 seconds.

Integrations: PayOS (8-second HTTP timeout, HMAC-SHA256 webhook), SMTP, and Gemini API (10-second connection timeout, 60-second request timeout). There is no code evidence of Kubernetes/Redis/CDN in this baseline.
Development runtime environment: the backend defaults to port 8080, the frontend defaults to port 3000; secrets are loaded from environment variables/.env.

Comparison baseline: the develop branch at commit 209b0e98a4d04028ed76dcdaa1551ad6be179542, together with the isBlocked fix in the login/JWT filter confirmed on July 22, 2026. When the code changes, the SRS must be reviewed against the new commit.
2.5. External Interfaces
Web UI: React/Vite calls REST APIs; authentication state is stored on the client and sent as a JWT Bearer token. Responsive and cross-browser behavior is verified in System Testing.

PayOS (A07): the backend creates a payment link using orderCode/amount/description/items/returnUrl/cancelUrl and HMAC-SHA256; receives webhooks at /api/webhooks/payos; supports verify/reconcile fallback; and grants Enrollment idempotently only after the Order is confirmed PAID.

Gemini API (A06): the backend calls generateContent; chat is limited to the latest 20 messages and is not stored in the DB; the roadmap produces 4-week JSON based on actual progress; timeout is 60 seconds and errors are returned in Vietnamese.

Notification/Email: OTPs and certain notifications use SMTP; the system provides in-app notifications for users/Admin and a broadcast endpoint. The current code does not implement push notifications or multi-channel scheduling.

Supabase Storage: private videos use signed URLs; avatars use public URLs; student documents are returned by the backend through unguessable one-time tokens; certificates are stored in the certificates bucket and returned through signed URLs valid for 600 seconds.
2.6. Constraints and Assumptions
Constraint: collected payment funds must be transferred directly to the company's account through PayOS, not to a Teacher's personal account.
Constraint: all transfers to Teachers must be performed manually by the Admin; no automation mechanism is provided.
Constraint: the system complies with Vietnam's Cybersecurity Law and the Personal Data Protection Decree (Decree 13/2023).

Constraint: the backend does not receive/store card data; users make payments on PayOS. AES-256 encryption for bank data must not be claimed unless the corresponding field-encryption mechanism exists in the baseline code.
Assumption: every account has a valid email address; the phone number is an optional profile field and, when provided, must follow the Vietnamese 10-digit format beginning with 03-09.

Assumption: PayOS and Gemini are available under the providers' service agreements; the backend must safely handle timeouts, API errors, and duplicate calls. No provider SLA is assigned when no contract is present in the repository.
Assumption: Parents can read and understand Vietnamese and have basic Internet-use skills.
3 FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

Each requirement is specified using a standard template comprising: Description, Actor, Preconditions, Main Flow, Exceptions / Alternative Flows, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Case mappings at all three levels (Unit - Integration - System). Each Requirement ID (REQ) maps one-to-one to a Use Case ID (UC) in Use Case Specification version 6.5. Version 4.18 prioritizes behavior backed by endpoints/services/entities in the develop baseline; SLAs/NFRs not guaranteed by code are explicitly stated as test objectives and are not treated as implemented functionality.
3.1. Authentication & Account Module

Module description: Students/Parents self-register through OTP; Teachers are provisioned by the Admin; authentication and tokens are provided by Supabase; the backend manages profiles, temporary login lockout, avatars, password change/reset, and Google OAuth synchronization.
3.2. Search & Course Module

Module description: Allows Guests and authenticated users to discover, search, and evaluate course information before purchase. This module optimizes the discovery experience and supports purchase conversion.
3.3. Purchase & Payment Module

Module description: Handles course purchase and payment through PayOS; manages Reward Points generated only from Exam results, the points wallet, and Reward Vouchers used to discount Orders. Payments go directly to the company account; the system records revenue_splits, and the Admin manually transfers funds at the end of each period.
3.4. Learning Module

Module description: Supports self-paced learning based on each Student's individual progress. There is no fixed class schedule. Each Exam unlocks automatically when the Student completes 100% of the content within the chapter range configured by the Teacher for that Exam under UC34. After completing the entire course and passing all 4 mandatory Exams, the system issues a PDF certificate with a verifiable QR code.
3.5. Interaction & Support Module

Module description: Thread-based Q&A between Students and Teachers; stateless Gemini learning chat; a 4-week JSON roadmap based on progress data; preliminary AI grading of Exams; and Quiz analysis based on the Student's attempt.
3.6. Parent Module

Module description: Supports Parents in accompanying their children's learning. Parent monitoring/actions (UC24, UC25, UC26) are available only when the Parent-Student link is ACTIVE. The linking mechanism consists of: sending an invitation (UC27) -> accepting/rejecting it (UC28) -> unlinking (UC29).
3.7. Teacher Module

Module description: Groups the operations available to Teachers, who are collaborating partners of the platform. Teachers manage their own course content, answer Q&A, grade submissions, update the question bank, and view the history of payout periods confirmed by the Admin; no real-time/estimated revenue screen is included in the current scope.
3.8. Admin Module

Module description: Provides tools for the Admin to operate the entire system, including a consolidated dashboard and trend analytics. The Admin is the only actor who transfers funds to Teachers - the system aggregates the figures, the Admin verifies them, performs the transfer manually, and confirms it in the system.
4 NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

The NFRs below distinguish two categories: (1) baseline behavior directly verifiable from the develop code; and (2) production targets that must be demonstrated through testing/deployment. An infrastructure target must not be considered implemented merely because it appears in the SRS.
5 BR - UC - REQ - TEST CASE TRACEABILITY MATRIX

The matrix below maps each Business Requirement (BR) group to the corresponding Use Cases (UC), Requirements (REQ), and test cases at Unit Test (UT), Integration Test (IT), and System Test (ST) levels.

Note: this matrix manages traceability at the ID level; detailed test-case content (steps, inputs, expected outputs, test data) is maintained in the corresponding Test Case Specification/Test Management Tool under the listed UT/IT/ST IDs.
BR conventions are provided in Appendix 6.1. Each REQ is associated with a BR group so testers can trace from the business objective to the test case.
NFR - Test Case Traceability Matrix (Supplementary)
5.1. Guidelines for Deriving Test Cases from the SRS
5.1.1. Unit Test
Objective: Test each function/method in the business-logic layer independently, using test drivers, stubs, and mocks to isolate the unit under test.
For each "Acceptance Criterion," create at least one happy-path test case.
For each "Exception / Alternative Flow," create at least one corresponding negative test case.
Apply Equivalence Partitioning and Boundary Value Analysis to fields with limits.
Example: UT-AUTH-001 tests the function validateEmail() with a valid input, a missing @, a missing domain, and special characters.
5.1.2. Integration Test
Objective: Test coordination among modules, APIs, the database, and external services. These tests typically run in a staging environment with a test database.
For each Main Flow, create an end-to-end integration test for that flow.
Test the API contract: correct HTTP status code, response body, and headers.
Mock external services (PayOS, Supabase Auth/Storage, Gemini, SMTP) to ensure test stability.
Example: IT-PAY-001 simulates a PayOS webhook with a valid signature -> verifies that the Order becomes PAID and revenue_splits are recorded correctly.
5.1.3. System Test
Objective: Test the entire system as a real user, including the UI, business logic, and real data. Selenium/Playwright/Cypress are typically used.
For each Use Case, create an end-to-end test scenario from start to finish.
Include UI, cross-browser, responsive, and accessibility testing.
Example: ST-AUTH-001 = open the registration page -> select Student/Parent -> request an OTP -> enter the OTP within 5 minutes and a valid password -> create the account -> log in successfully.

This appendix restores the Section 7 components present in SRS 4.15 but missing from the English Version 4.18 file. It is not a copy of the old appendix: all obsolete assumptions (VNPay/MoMo, microservices, Redis/CDN, public Teacher registration, Parent-created Orders, refund workflow, role-changing rules, and unimplemented schedulers) have been removed. 7.2. Product Context and System Boundary

The repository does not implement a microservice/Kubernetes/Redis/CDN architecture, automatic refunds, automatic Teacher transfers, Parent-created Orders, or a fixed class schedule.

Initial SRS document for Bee Academy, including 8 modules, 44 use cases, 5 primary actors, and 2 external systems.

Resolved SRS review issues: standardized actor/BR traceability, RBAC, state machines, payment/refund/payout, teacher onboarding, assignments/deadlines, AI privacy, NFRs, external interface contracts, and test-case IDs.

Resolved remaining review issues: standardized User/Teacher and Payment/Reconciliation states, added webhook validation, clarified watch_time, content versioning, chapter-count conditions, essay grading, RBAC/traceability, and external interfaces.

Resolved remaining review issues: standardized Parent Link, Payment Review, lesson-completion rules, certificate/final-exam rules, payout-item statuses, temporary login lockout, secure password reset, NFR backup/security, and NFR traceability.
Additional fixes: payment retry/payment_attempt, partial refund/access state, Teacher bank-account review, Parent-Student privacy consent, Teacher module, and payout preconditions.
Additional fixes: synchronized the payment_attempt webhook, standardized payment_status/refund_status, Teacher bank-account status, and load-test thresholds.
Additional fixes: narrowed Appendix tables 6.3-6.5 to prevent margin clipping, added score-correction auditing, separated account_status/teacher_profile_state, and clarified Q&A boundaries.
An unauthenticated user who may access public pages such as course search, course details, and free preview lessons.

Inherits Guest capabilities. Purchases courses, learns at an individual pace, takes Quizzes/Exams, earns points from Exams, redeems and applies Reward Vouchers, receives preliminary AI grading/attempt analysis, reviews courses, and is issued, views, and downloads certificates.

Inherits Guest capabilities. Sends link invitations, monitors progress, exports XLSX progress reports, views the payment history of a linked child, and communicates with Teachers. The current code does not allow a Parent to create an Order on behalf of a child.

The account is provisioned by the Admin with a temporary password and must change the password. The Teacher creates/manages courses, Quizzes/Exams, the question bank, grading, retakes, Q&A, bank information, and their own revenue/payout data.
Platform administrator. Approves courses, handles complaints, operates manual payout cycles for Teachers, views dashboards and analytics, sends notifications, and manages users.

Google Generative Language service called by the backend for learning chat, a 4-week roadmap, preliminary AI grading of Exams/Quiz analysis for Students, and AI-assisted Teacher tasks; it is not an internal Bee Academy microservice.
PayOS. Receives payment transactions and sends confirmation webhooks to the Bee Academy backend.
A Student or Parent self-registers an account using an email address, full name, password, and role; Teachers do not register publicly and are provisioned by the Admin under REQ-ADM-003.
The email address has no existing profile in the system. The SMTP service is available, or dev mode is enabled in the development environment.

1. The Guest selects the student or parent role and enters an email address and full name.
2. The frontend calls POST /api/auth/register/request-otp.
3. The backend validates the role/email, generates a 6-digit OTP, and stores it in memory for 5 minutes.
4. The Guest enters the OTP and a password of at least 8 characters containing at least 1 uppercase letter and 1 digit.
5. The frontend calls POST /api/auth/register/verify-otp.
6. The backend verifies the OTP, creates a Supabase Auth user and a Profile with the same UUID, and then consumes the OTP.
7. The user can log in immediately. The /api/auth/register endpoint remains as a legacy-compatible direct-registration flow but is not the preferred UI flow.

E1: A role other than student/parent -> INVALID_ROLE.
E2: Email already exists -> EMAIL_ALREADY_EXISTS.
E3: OTP does not exist, is incorrect, or has exceeded 5 minutes -> OTP_NOT_FOUND / OTP_INVALID / OTP_EXPIRED.
E4: SMTP fails in production -> MAIL_SEND_FAILED; in dev mode, the OTP is logged only for development.
E5: Supabase user or Profile creation fails -> do not return success; auth/profile inconsistency must be handled as an operational incident.

AC1: Public signup accepts only student or parent.
AC2: The OTP is 6 digits, has a TTL of 300 seconds, each email retains only the newest entry, and the OTP is single-use.
AC3: Password validation matches regex ^(?=.*[A-Z])(?=.*\d).{8,}$.
AC4: Profile.id matches the Supabase auth user ID; passwords are not stored in the profiles table.
AC5: Teachers are created by the Admin through a separate endpoint and have mustChangePassword=true.
The user logs in with email/password through Supabase GoTrue; the backend adds profile/role information to AuthTokenResponse.
Student (A02), Parent (A03), Teacher (A04), Admin (A05) | Type: Functional | Priority: High
The Supabase account and Profile exist; Profile.isBlocked=false; the account is not temporarily locked due to failed login attempts.

1. The user calls POST /api/auth/login.
2. The backend locates the Profile and rejects login if Profile.isBlocked=true.
3. The backend checks failed_login_lock_until.
4. The backend calls Supabase signInWithPassword.
5. On success, the failed-attempt count is reset; the response contains accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn, tokenType, and a user object enriched from the Profile.
6. For every protected request, JwtAuthenticationFilter verifies the JWT, checks Profile.isBlocked, and rejects the request before it reaches the controller if the account has been blocked.
7. The frontend stores the token and routes the user according to role.
8. When the access token expires, the frontend calls POST /api/auth/refresh.

E1: Invalid credentials -> INVALID_CREDENTIALS; if the Profile exists, increment failedLoginAttempts.
E2: 5 failed attempts within a 15-minute window -> temporary lockout for 15 minutes.
E3: Profile not found after Supabase login -> still return the original AuthTokenResponse to avoid a null error; profile synchronization must be handled operationally.
E4: Profile.isBlocked=true at login time -> the backend returns HTTP 403 and does not issue an accessToken or refreshToken.
E5: The account is blocked by the Admin after a JWT has already been issued -> JwtAuthenticationFilter returns HTTP 403 for protected requests; the request is not passed to the controller/service.

AC1: Authentication is performed by Supabase; the backend does not compare password hashes itself.
AC2: The JWT contains/is enriched with the required role and profile information.
AC3: The temporary lockout rule of 5 attempts/15 minutes is code-backed and separate from isBlocked.
AC4: Token validity is taken from the provider response and is not hard-coded as 24 hours/7 days in the SRS.
AC5: The refresh token is exchanged through /api/auth/refresh.
AC6: Login rejects Profile.isBlocked=true with HTTP 403 and returns no token.
AC7: JwtAuthenticationFilter rejects every protected request from a blocked account, even when the JWT has not expired.
The user logs out of the current session by requesting Supabase sign-out and deleting client-side tokens.
Student (A02), Parent (A03), Teacher (A04), Admin (A05) | Type: Functional | Priority: Medium
A valid JWT Bearer token is available.

1. The frontend calls POST /api/auth/logout with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>.
2. The controller validates CurrentUser and extracts the raw token.
3. AuthService calls Supabase signOut.
4. The frontend deletes the access/refresh tokens from local state/localStorage and returns to a public page.

E1: Missing/invalid JWT -> 401.
E2: Network failure -> the client must still delete local tokens; the server session may remain until the provider processes it or the token expires.

AC1: The provider-side refresh/session is revoked when the request succeeds.
AC2: An issued access JWT may remain valid until the Supabase TTL expires; the SRS does not describe an access-token blacklist when the code has none.
AC3: The frontend no longer sends the token after logout.
The user resets the password using a 5-minute email OTP; the Supabase recovery-link endpoint remains available for compatibility.
The user has access to the registered email account.

1. The user calls POST /api/auth/reset-password/request-otp with the email address.
2. The backend always returns a neutral message and sends an OTP only if the email exists.
3. The user sends email, otp, and newPassword to POST /api/auth/reset-password/verify-otp.
4. The backend verifies the OTP, finds the user ID, updates the password through the Supabase Admin API, and consumes the OTP.
5. In addition, POST /api/auth/reset-password can send a Supabase recovery email.

E1: Email does not exist -> do not disclose this and do not send an OTP.
E2: OTP is incorrect/expired/missing -> return the corresponding error.
E3: SMTP or Supabase fails -> return a service error and do not falsely report a successful reset.

AC1: The reset OTP is 6 digits, has a 5-minute TTL, and is single-use.
AC2: The request endpoint prevents email enumeration.
AC3: The new password must pass request/provider validation.
AC4: A history of 3 passwords or a 60-minute reset link is not required if the baseline code does not implement it.
The user views/updates their own profile, uploads an avatar, and changes the password; Teacher bank operations and Parent-Student consent have separate endpoints.
Student (A02), Parent (A03), Teacher (A04) | Type: Functional | Priority: Medium

1. GET /api/me returns the CurrentUser's Profile.
2. PUT /api/me updates optional fields individually: fullName, phone, bio, twitterUrl, facebookUrl, linkedinUrl; null means retain the current value.
3. POST /api/me/avatar receives multipart field file; the service validates MIME/size, uploads to Supabase Storage, and saves avatarUrl.
4. POST /api/auth/change-password receives the current/new password and calls the provider.
5. GET /api/me/courses returns the list of enrolled courses.
6. A Teacher changes the bank account through /api/teacher/bank/change-requests + /verify; a Student manages consent through /api/student/parent-link-invitations/{parentId}/sensitive-data-consent.

E1: Invalid field format -> 400 field errors.
E2: phone does not match 0[3-9][0-9]{8} -> reject.
E3: Invalid social-media URL -> reject.
E4: Avatar has an invalid MIME type/size or upload fails -> do not change avatarUrl.
E5: User attempts to submit id/role/email -> the DTO has no such fields, and id is always taken from the JWT.

AC1: Update only the CurrentUser's Profile to prevent IDOR.
AC2: Email and role cannot be changed through PUT /api/me.
AC3: fullName is 2-100 characters; bio is at most 500 characters.
AC4: Bank information is not included in UpdateProfileRequest.
AC5: Parent-Student consent is not included in UpdateProfileRequest.
Guests and authenticated users search the list of PUBLISHED courses using filters and pagination.
Guest (A01), Student (A02), Parent (A03), Teacher (A04), Admin (A05) | Type: Functional | Priority: High
At least one PUBLISHED course exists, or the system returns a valid empty page.

1. The client calls GET /api/courses with optional queries: subject, grade, q, featured, minPrice, maxPrice, minRating, page, size, sort.
2. The backend always applies onlyPublished.
3. The keyword is lowercased, diacritics are removed, it is split into at most 6 tokens, and matched against title/description/slug/category/teacher/grade.
4. The system sorts by relevance/newest/best_selling/price_asc/price_desc/rating.
5. PageResponse defaults to size 20 and includes rating, review count, student count, and a preview flag.

E1: No results -> return empty items with page metadata.
E2: Empty filter -> return the newest PUBLISHED courses.
E3: Invalid min/max price or minRating -> ignore it or return validation according to the controller/service.

AC1: The public API does not return DRAFT/PENDING_REVIEW/NEEDS_REVISION/REJECTED/ARCHIVED courses.
AC2: Search supports Vietnamese with or without diacritics and prioritizes relevance when q is provided.
AC3: The effective price is salePriceVnd when present; otherwise, priceVnd.

2. The service verifies the student role, course existence, and lack of ownership.
3. The subtotal is calculated using salePriceVnd when present; otherwise, priceVnd.
4. Create a PENDING Order and OrderItem price snapshots.
5. If a valid voucher is provided, reserve it for the Order and reduce the amount by min(subtotal, discountAmount).
6. If totalAmount <= 0, mark it PAID immediately; otherwise, call PayOS to create checkoutUrl/paymentLinkId with an 8-second timeout.

E2: Course missing -> 404; already enrolled -> ALREADY_ENROLLED.
E3: Voucher invalid/not applicable -> VOUCHER_NOT_AVAILABLE/VOUCHER_NOT_APPLICABLE.

The system confirms PayOS payment through a webhook or verify/reconcile fallback, grants Enrollment, and records revenue splits idempotently.
Student (A02), PayOS (A07) | Type: Functional | Priority: High
The Order exists and is normally PENDING; if a voucher is used, it is RESERVED for the Order.

1. PayOS returns checkoutUrl/paymentLinkId when the link is created.
2. Webhook POST /api/webhooks/payos receives data + signature, canonicalizes exactly 9 fields, and verifies HMAC-SHA256.
3. code="00" calls handlePayOSWebhook(orderCode).
4. The service locks/checks the PENDING Order, creates Enrollment, records the revenue split, changes the Order to PAID, and changes the voucher to USED.
5. The Student may POST /api/orders/{id}/verify or /api/orders/reconcile if the webhook does not arrive.
6. POST /api/orders/{id}/cancel checks PayOS status before cancellation; reconciliation changes the Order to CANCELLED/EXPIRED and releases the voucher.

E1: Invalid signature in production -> ACK without processing; dev mode has a development-only bypass that must be disabled on deployment.
E2: Webhook ping lacks orderCode or code is not 00 -> ignore safely.
E3: Order is no longer PENDING/duplicate webhook -> do not create a second Enrollment.
E4: PayOS is already PAID when the user requests cancellation -> process as PAID rather than CANCELLED.
E5: Network failure during verify/reconcile -> retain the current state and retry later.

AC1: The webhook always ACKs {error:0,message:"success"} to prevent endless PayOS retries.
AC2: Enrollment and revenue splits are not duplicated.
AC3: CANCELLED/EXPIRED releases a RESERVED voucher; PAID changes it to USED.
AC4: Payment-link creation timeout is 8 seconds.
AC5: The baseline code does not implement automatic refund_status/refund_request; they are not part of the Main Flow.

AC1: Prevent IDOR by deriving userId from the JWT.

A Student receives Reward Points from the effective Exam score; each Exam records only the best result, and a retake awards only the increase.
studentId, assessmentType EXAM, assessmentId, and effective scorePercent have been determined. An Exam containing essay questions is rewarded only after the effective score is finalized.

1. When an Exam has a valid effective score, the service calls recordAssessmentScore.
2. Normalize scorePercent to 0-100 and round it to points.
3. Find StudentRewardSource by studentId + assessmentType EXAM + assessmentId.
4. New source: save best score/awardedPoints and award the full amount.
5. Existing source: update only if the new score is higher and award the delta.
6. Increase availablePoints and lifetimePoints.

E1: Missing identifier -> return 0 and award nothing.
E2: NaN/Infinity -> 0 points.
E3: New score is not higher -> delta 0.
E4: Essay Exam has no effective score yet -> Reward Points are not finalized.
E5: assessmentType other than EXAM -> rejected by the current model/constraint.

AC1: Only assessmentType EXAM may create a reward source.
AC2: Reward points per source are within 0-100.
AC3: Points cannot be farmed by resubmitting the same Exam with a lower/equal score.
AC4: availablePoints and lifetimePoints both increase when points are awarded.

3. When creating an Order, the correct Student's AVAILABLE voucher is reserved, and discount=min(subtotal,discountAmount).
4. Order PAID -> USED; CANCELLED/EXPIRED -> release to AVAILABLE.

E1: Voucher catalog item does not exist/is inactive -> 404/VOUCHER_INACTIVE.
E2: Insufficient points -> NOT_ENOUGH_POINTS.
E3: Student voucher has the wrong owner/status -> VOUCHER_NOT_AVAILABLE.
E4: Discount <=0 -> VOUCHER_NOT_APPLICABLE.

AC2: Redemption is a transaction that subtracts points and creates a voucher.
AC3: lifetimePoints does not decrease.
AC4: One Order may use at most one voucher.
AC5: USED vouchers cannot be released or reused.

The system evaluates eligibility, issues a PDF/QR certificate, reissues or revokes it, and supports public verification.
Student (A02); Guest/any person for verification | Type: Functional | Priority: High
The Student is enrolled; course progress exists; the course version contains all Exam slots 0-3.

1. After progress/scores change, the service may call tryIssueAfterProgress; the Student may also POST /api/student/courses/{courseId}/certificate.
2. Eligibility requires completion of every progress item and PASSED status for all 4 required Exam configurations in slots 0-3.
3. Select the best passed attempt for slot 3 as finalExamAttempt.
4. Generate a landscape A4 PDF containing the name, course, Teacher, final score, date, certificateNo, QR code, and watermark; upload it to the certificates bucket.
5. The first issuance is ISSUED; reissuance is REISSUED and increments version; signed URLs are valid for 600 seconds.
6. Public GET /api/certificates/verify/{verificationCode} returns information/status.
7. When a Teacher changes the score of a required Exam: an issued certificate becomes NEEDS_REVIEW; if eligibility remains satisfied, reissue it; otherwise, set it to REVOKED.

E2: Progress/4 Exams incomplete -> CERTIFICATE_NOT_ELIGIBLE.

AC4: The QR code points to the frontend verification URL.
AC5: Score changes may result in REISSUED or REVOKED.
